programmes.show_description: The Tiger and the Fox: Ah Sahm is faced with a dilemma when Bolo is sent to hunt down Long Zii and Mai Ling. Meanwhile, Lee's past catches up with him. (S1, ep 7)
Series shining a new light on modern policing in Manchester and the challenges faced by officers as they come face to face with a range of criminal and social issues. (S1, ep 1)
Explosive documentary following Lincolnshire cops as they fight crime across one of the UK's largest counties. (S5, ep 4)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 5)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 6)

Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they pull over motorists for a variety of suspected offences. (S21, ep 1)

A Russian dissident's complicated past raises questions, and officers wonder why a Korean tourist would bring his CV and school diplomas on a vacation. (S1, ep 11)
The Inland Enforcement Unit tracks a pair of Honduran drug runners, while an American tourist seems to have already put down roots in Canada. (S1, ep 12)
A smuggling attempt unfolds at the Brisbane Container Examination Facility. Meanwhile, a passenger's unusual behaviour raises alarm bells. (S17, ep 11)
Identical packages at the mail centre raise alarm bells, a passenger goes missing during a bag search and a young man is distraught after being caught. (S17, ep 12)
Officers interrogate a popular woman, a religious figurine may be concealing something sinister and a student from China is on the brink of being sent home. (S17, ep 13)
A man is raising red flags when he arrives in Brisbane. A woman tries to smuggle dangerous goods and officers find some strong 'tea' at the air cargo facility. (S17, ep 14)

A Dutch woman with a lot of luggage claims she's on a four-day holiday, while a Chinese parcel contains a shocking surprise. Corey Feldman shows up too. (S1, ep 2)
An angry businessman hides the real reason for his hostility and officers try to process some unusual Mexican migrants. (S1, ep 3)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S8, ep 5)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S8, ep 6)
Follow Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the country's quarantine, customs, immigration and finance laws. (S16, ep 20)
In Sydney, Border Force officers are worried about a young girl who has met a man online, while a shipment of lollies is not what it seems. (S17, ep 1)
Biosecurity makes a gruesome discovery, officers interrogate a couple whose stories aren't stacking up and a couple are caught importing dangerous goods. (S17, ep 2)
A traveller has romance on his mind but may be hiding something from Border Force officers. A passenger arriving from Africa is caught with high-risk items. (S17, ep 3)
The Storyteller: Jake Sisko and Nog fight over a young Bajoran girl. Also, An elderly Bajoran transfers his powers to O'Brien before dying, and without explaining how to use them.
The Devil You Know - Part II: SG-1 must escape from a prison built to simulate the fires of Hell. (S3, ep 13)
Drive: Peter and Kay enter a race symbolising the signing of a momentous peace treaty, and B'Elanna fears her relationship with Tom isn't meant to be. (S7, ep 3)
Bloodlines: A Ferengi plans to avenge the death of his son by killing Picard's son.
Concrete Menagerie: Stephen reluctantly helps Lester capture a Silurian scorpion. The conspiracy unravels and people working on the project die - or do they? Season finale. (S2, ep 7)
Carentan: An uncomfortable truth resonates for the Easy Company as they battle on against the Germans, their injuries and their fears. Contains flashing images. (3 of 10)
Replacements: A rest in England prepares Easy Co and their replacements for a mission. An attack serves as a sobering initiation for the new recruits. Contains flashing images. (4 of 10)
El Cuegle: As Nick and Hank investigate the kidnapping of a baby boy, a young family are terrorised by a frightening new Wesen. (S6, ep 4)
They Don't Pay Us Enough to Think: A bloodbath on Chinatown's streets leads the Hop Wei and Long Xii to consider an unusual way to end the hostilities. (S1, ep 8)

Trouble looms for a young Asian woman carrying someone else's bag, and a pair of Canadians return from a concert in California with more than a T-shirt. (S1, ep 13)
Packaged sweets leave a sour taste at JFK Airport and it's a case of mistaken identity for one traveller in Washington. (S1, ep 1)
A woman brings contents of her fridge with her, a suspicious clock at the mail centre has officers worried and a man returning from Cambodia raises alarms. (S17, ep 15)
Officers uncover a smuggling effort at the mail centre, two passengers from Taiwan are caught lying and packages of undeclared kava raise officers' suspicions. (S17, ep 16)
A drunk passenger has officers on high alert, a box of clothing may be hiding something sinister and officers interrogate a student about fraudulent documents. (S17, ep 17)
A travel guide makes a mistake that could cost him thousands, officers intercept a strange shipment and the legitimacy of a student's visa comes into question. (S17, ep 18)

Officers drill an American oil worker with a shady past, and a British traveller's proposed reunion with a girlfriend could be cut short. (S1, ep 4)
A sloshed snowboarder causes trouble at the airport, before a pair of packages add up to a deadly surprise. (S1, ep 5)

An Uber driver tries to smuggle prohibited goods, a suspicious shipment of football gear piques officers' interest and an unusual snack alarms officers. (S17, ep 4)
At Sydney Airport, a routine inspection takes an extreme turn. Plus, Officers are suspicious of a woman with a 'borrowed' suitcase. (S17, ep 5)
An unhappy passenger causes trouble for Border Force in Perth. Meanwhile, officers uncover a million-dollar smuggling attempt in a shipping container. (S17, ep 6)
Officers find disturbing information on a Russian woman's phone, a family are caught red-handed by Border Force, and officers make an alarming discovery. (S17, ep 7)
Progress: The Bajoran government decides to mine one of its moons for badly needed resources, but Major Kira Nerys finds herself opposed to her people on environmental grounds.
Foothold: The team returns from a mission to find that no one at the Stargate Command is who they appear to be. (S3, ep 14)
Repression: Tuvok takes charge and investigates when former members of the Maquis aboard the ship are targeted in a series of strange attacks. (S7, ep 4)
Emergence: After a series of unexplained mechanical malfunctions occur on the Enterprise, Data suspects that the ship has begun thinking for itself.
Crocodile Fears: As the team mourns the loss of Stephen Hart, Lester recruits Captain Becker to prevent more deaths. A creature wreaks havoc at the British Museum. (S3, ep 1)
Murder in Silence: The case of Suzanne Greenhill, a deaf woman murdered in her own home, whose murder took forensic investigators on a trail through Europe. (S2, ep 8)

programmes.show_name: FILM: For A Few Dollars More
programmes.show_description: Two bounty killers with similar intentions but different motivations team up to take down a psychotic Mexican outlaw before he and his gang can carry out an audacious bank robbery. (1965)
